reasoning: `lemma_write_seq_idle` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the frame property (writes to disjoint addresses don't affect reads) requires an inductive proof over `fold_left` that is not verified. This is a key lemma used by `lemma_valid_implies_equal_reads`.

This is a transitive trust assumption — `lemma_valid_implies_equal_reads` is a verified proof, but its body calls `lemma_write_seq_idle` which is `external_body`. The TSO consistency guarantee is only as strong as the unverified frame lemma.

`contains_fst` is defined as `exists|i| 0 <= i < self.len() && self[i] == (fst, self[i].1)`. If `self[i] == (fst, self[i].1)`, then `self[i].0 == fst` by tuple projection. This is a correct logical consequence of the definition.

This is the straightforward contrapositive of `inv_mapping__valid_is_not_in_sbuf`: the invariant says `P_bit_set ==> !in_sbuf`, so `in_sbuf ==> !P_bit_set`. This is a correct logical transformation, not a spec gap.
